slides.title: Describing People and Things
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 6: Describing People and Things. Welcome to Unit 6! In this unit you will learn how to describe people and things. You will learn words for what a person is like on the inside — such as kind, honest and friendly — and words for what things are like on the outside — such as rough, tough and smooth. You will describe your favourite people, compare two things, and tell true stories about the past. By the end, you will be ready to enjoy this unit's story about Nadia, a new girl who learns that carrying kindness makes everyone feel welcome. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use describing words (adjectives) to talk about people and things
Describe your favourite person and say what makes them special
Compare two people or things, using words like kinder and rougher than
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use describing words (adjectives) to talk about people and things. you will also describe your favourite person and say what makes them special. you will also compare two people or things, using words like kinder and rougher than. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: kind, friendly, honest, kindness, care, busy. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes My Cousin Ishmael and Two Roads. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: The Past Simple Tense
bullets: We use the past simple to talk about things that already happened. It helps us tell stories and describe what people did — like Hannah, who helped the boy...
slides.narration: The first language focus is The Past Simple Tense. We use the past simple to talk about things that already happened. It helps us tell stories and describe what people did — like Hannah, who helped the boy who fell. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1 — A Description of a Person
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Describe Your Favourite Person. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
